_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
H2Accelerate TRUCKS creates no registration or reporting obligation for freight forwarders. The workspace has no filing, enrolment, or data-submission requirement under Grant Agreement 101101446 or under any associated EU regulatory instrument arising directly from this initiative.

The registration and reporting landscape relevant to the workspace derives from parallel instruments. Under AFIR (Regulation (EU) 2023/1804, in force 13 April 2024), the reporting obligations fall entirely on EU member states (National Policy Frameworks, due 1 January 2026) and on operators of hydrogen refuelling points (price transparency, ad hoc pricing per kg displayed publicly). Neither obligation touches the workspace.

Under Regulation (EU) 2024/1610 (HDV CO2 standards amendment), the monitored and reporting entities are vehicle manufacturers, not freight forwarders or transport buyers. The workspace's road carrier partners bear any downstream compliance documentation burden.

The one registration-adjacent area requiring the workspace's attention is data collection for client Scope 3 sustainability reporting. If clients operating under CSRD begin requesting carrier-level GHG intensity data for road freight — specifically gCO2e per tonne-kilometre for intra-EU road moves — the workspace will need to be able to disaggregate carrier fuel type and emission intensity from its transport management records. This is not a current regulatory mandate on the workspace but is a commercially driven data requirement that is reasonable to anticipate before 2030. The workspace should confirm: (a) which road carrier partners are capturing vehicle-level fuel type and emission data; (b) whether those carriers' data systems are compatible with the EU transport GHG accounting methodology proposed under COM(2023) 441 (political agreement reached November 2025, implementing rules pending); and (c) what fields in the workspace's own transport management system would need to be populated to generate lane-level GHG intensity reports.

No published registration format, portal, or form for freight forwarder enrolment in the H2Accelerate programme has been identified — consistent with the project structure, which places trucks with OEM-selected operators rather than through open procurement.

*Source: Clean Hydrogen Partnership Project Dashboard, H2Accelerate TRUCKS. https://www.clean-hydrogen.europa.eu/projects-dashboard/projects-repository/h2accelerate-trucks_en. Regulation (EU) 2023/1804 (AFIR), EUR-Lex. https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX:32023R1804. EC DG MOVE, political agreement on transport GHG accounting, November 2025. https://transport.ec.europa.eu/news-events/news/commission-welcomes-political-agreement-new-rules-harmonise-transport-emissions-calculations-eu-2025-11-06_en.*
